description:
First step that actually affects the goldens. Applies .21.1's three
coordinated codegen sites without the earlier force-Tagged patches.

Touch src/ir_codegen.rs:

1. build_fn_signature cont-fn branch: drop `let _ = param_reprs`,
   use param_reprs[0].cl_type() for the result param. Self stays i64.

2. compile_fn cont-fn entry harness: append_block_param(my_param_reprs[0]
   .cl_type()) instead of forced i64; drop the always-Tagged coerce on
   params[0]. The Cranelift param IS already in the body's expected repr.

3. Term::Return native branch: build sig with
   AbiParam::new(return_reprs[this_spec_id].cl_type()) for the value
   slot; coerce val to my_return_repr (was forced Tagged).

4. Print builtin lowering (line ~4818): check raw_int_vars /
   raw_f64_vars in addition to descr_is_int / descr_is_float, so cont
   bodies whose param[0] was narrowed by typer effective_return_descr
   route to the typed FFI even when the typer Descr stayed Any.

## Reference: add1.clif

The current golden has:

  add1_s2:
    v4 = ishl_imm v3, 3    ← these two go
    v5 = bor_imm v4, 1
    v6 = load.i64 v1+16
    return_call_indirect sig0, v6(v5, v1)   ← becomes v6(v3, v1)

  k_2_s3:
    v2 = sshr_imm v0, 3   ← goes
    call fn0(v2)          ← becomes fn0(v0)

That's the visible delete this ticket should produce in add1.clif.

## What might break

Closure-bearing fixtures (higher_order, closure_typed_captures, etc.)
have a forced-Tagged closure-target ABI that the cont seam used to
absorb via the universal Tagged coerce. With .21.1, those seams may
mismatch — the closure-target body returns Tagged-forced, the cont
keyed against the typer's narrow Descr, and the producer's Term::Return
trusts the typer's narrow return_repr. Mismatch.

The follow-up tickets 22.1 / 22.2 / 22.3 drop the closure-target
force and wire typed CallClosure. Until then, expect closure-bearing
fixtures to either:
  * pass anyway (typer happened to widen them already, no narrow seam
    to break), OR
  * fail with the producer/consumer mismatch — recorded in the golden
    diff as suspicious instructions.

The golden_clif test will reveal the per-fixture impact precisely.

## Acceptance

- add1.clif diff: the three deleted instructions described above.
- tail_recursion.clif diff: clean elision at count's cont seam (the
  same shape as add1).
- Other goldens: review diffs. Bless intentional changes. Fail and
  iterate on unintentional ones.
- Test suite halt-value tests: green for fixtures without closures.
  Closure-bearing tests may regress; record which and how.
- If closure-bearing regressions block landing, the ticket scope
  expands to include 22.1 + 22.2 + 22.3 atomically.
title: fz-cps-closure.4: typed Term::Return cont seam
